en.extract:
Taita white-eye (Zosterops silvanus) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It includes numerous subspecies, sometimes considered a subspecies to montane white-eye (Z. poliogastrus). It is only found in Taita Hills in southeastern Kenya. IUCN categorizes it as vulnerable. It is threatened by severe habitat loss and fragmentation, mainly as a result of the deforestation of the Taita Hills.
It breeds in cloud forests.
It is often observed in mixed flocks with the pale white-eye.

'''Taita white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops silvanus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It includes numerous [[subspecies]], sometimes considered a subspecies to [[montane white-eye]] (''Z. poliogastrus''). It is only found in [[Taita Hills]] in southeastern [[Kenya]]. [[IUCN]] categorizes it as vulnerable.<ref name="iucn status 9 December 2022" /> It is threatened by severe habitat loss and fragmentation, mainly as a result of the deforestation of the Taita Hills.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Vedder |first1=Daniel |last2=Lens |first2=Luc |last3=Martin |first3=Claudia A. |last4=Pellikka |first4=Petri |last5=Adhikari |first5=Hari |last6=Heiskanen |first6=Janne |last7=Engler |first7=Jan O. |last8=Sarmento Cabral |first8=Juliano |date=July 2022 |title=Hybridization may aid evolutionary rescue of an endangered East African passerine |journal=Evolutionary Applications |volume=15 |issue=7 |pages=1177–1188 |doi=10.1111/eva.13440 |pmid=35899253 |pmc=9309464 |bibcode=2022EvApp..15.1177V |issn=1752-4571|hdl=1854/LU-01H05KG780T68Z9E9WH2758MBN |hdl-access=free }}</ref>

It breeds in [[Cloud forest|cloud forests]].<ref name=":0" />

It is often observed in mixed flocks with the [[pale white-eye]].<ref name=":0" />
